title: Chrysopsis latifolia
intro: Chrysopsis latifolia, also known as Bay Goldenrod, is a perennial plant species native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its yellow flowers and broad, oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Chrysopsis latifolia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Chrysopsis latifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the United States and is also known as the broad-leaved goldeneye. This plant is a shrub-like perennial with yellow flowers that are an important food source for various pollinators. Chrysopsis latifolia is further classified within the genus Chrysopsis, which is a group of flowering plants native to eastern North America. The species is also known by the synonyms Heterotheca latifolia and Heterotheca subulifolia, although some taxonomic sources may not recognize these synonyms.
uses: Chrysopsis latifolia, also known as broad-leaved goldeneye, has been used in traditional medicine. It is valued for its anti-inflammatory properties and has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. Some studies suggest that Chrysopsis latifolia may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential remedy for infections and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Chrysopsis latifolia as a medicinal plant.

The plant is known to contain flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
